content:
If you have ever built projects on AWS, participated in a hackathon, or studied for AWS certifications, you've likely experienced the dread of opening an email to find a surprise $50–$200 AWS bill. 💸

The worst part? The charges often come from hidden resources hiding in regions you rarely use.

Common culprits that quietly drain your wallet include:

•
NAT Gateways (~$32/month each, even with zero traffic!)

•
Unattached EBS Volumes (left behind after deleting an EC2 instance)

•
Unassociated Elastic IPs (AWS charges you when an EIP is not attached to a running instance!)

• Orphaned Load Balancers & RDS Databases

🛠️ The Solution: AWS Terminator

To solve this across all 34 AWS regions without manually clicking through every region in the AWS Management Console, check out AWS Terminator—a lightweight, single-file Python CLI utility.

Repository: github.com/abhinandnm/aws-terminator

✨ Key Features

•
Multi-Region Coverage: Automatically audits all 34 active AWS regions in seconds.

•
Cost Explorer Integration: Displays your current accrued monthly bill before scanning.

•
Resource Name Tags: Shows human-readable Name tags alongside resource IDs (e.g., i-060377c3e7fe389fe (hackathon-test)).

•
Safety Confirmation: Performs a complete dry-run scan first and requires typing yes before performing any deletion.

•
Smart Error Handling: Distinguishes between initialising Cost Explorer data and real IAM permission errors.

🚀 Quick Start

For Windows Users (Easiest):

• Download the latest release zip from GitHub Releases.

• Extract the ZIP folder.

•
Double-click run.bat (it automatically checks dependencies and launches the script).

For Mac / Linux Users:

• Download the release or clone the repository:

bash
git clone https://github.com/abhinandnm/aws-terminator.git
cd aws-terminator

Enter fullscreen mode

Exit fullscreen mode
